repo_notes.content:
This is constructive-airsim-ms-python, the Constructive component of the TCC LVC Smart Cities simulation platform. It is a Python microservice that controls an LLM-driven drone flying inside Microsoft AirSim (Unreal Engine, CityEnviron map) over the Mackenzie Higienópolis campus in São Paulo. It represents the 'C' in LVC — simulated agents operating simulated systems, generating synthetic events that enrich the simulation scenario.

Stack: Python 3.11+ (language, asyncio), Microsoft AirSim (drone simulator, Unreal Engine), FastAPI (REST API), gRPC (event publishing and subscription), NVIDIA NIM APIs (cloud LLM inference for flight planning), Ollama Gemma 4 / gemma4:e2b (local LLM alternative), Protocol Buffers (event contracts), Docker (containerization).

Core architecture: two parallel execution contexts — (1) AirSimControlThread running a synchronous 10 Hz control loop for drone movement, collision detection, and telemetry; (2) the asyncio main loop hosting FastAPI, LLM calls, and gRPC publishers. They communicate only via asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(), ensuring thread safety without locks.

LLM-driven flight: the LLM (NVIDIA NIM or Ollama) generates full JSON flight plans (~60 DroneMove objects) based on behavior-specific system prompts with spatial context injection. A code-enforced spatial guardrail overrides the LLM when the drone exceeds altitude (38 m) or patrol radius (400 m) bounds. The drone also executes bus stop photo missions triggered by BusApproachEvent from live-ms-java via gRPC.

Event integration: three outbound gRPC publishers (CrashPublisher, BusPhotoPublisher, AreaPublisher) send events to the Event Hub. One inbound gRPC server (DroneControllerServicer) receives BusApproachEvent from live-ms-java and enqueues a photo mission. All gRPC is suppressed when test_mode=True.

Key architectural patterns: Dual-Loop Architecture (sync control + async I/O), LLM-as-Planner (AI-generated flight plans with safety guardrails), Publish/Subscribe (gRPC event integration with Event Hub), Priority-Based Control (bus mission > spatial guardrail > LLM queue > hover).
repo_notes.author: Rafael Pereira

pages.title: Architecture Overview
pages.purpose: High-level overview of the Constructive component's role in the LVC platform. Explain the dual-loop architecture (AirSimControlThread vs asyncio main loop), how they communicate via run_coroutine_threadsafe, and the overall data flow from LLM plan generation to drone movement to event publishing. Cover the technology stack, the Mackenzie Higienópolis coordinate system (NED with campus as origin), and how this component integrates with the Event Hub and other LVC components.

pages.title: LLM Flight Plan Pipeline
pages.purpose: Document the AI-powered flight planning system. Cover: MoveQueue.maybe_replan trigger threshold, LLMPolicy.generate_plan calling NVIDIA NIM or Ollama, the behavior-specific system prompts in prompts.py with spatial context injection, the DroneMove JSON schema, the _clamp_move safety envelope, fallback to traced_route on timeout or empty plan, the LLM warmup sequence at startup, and the DEFAULT_PLAN_SUMMARY injected into every system prompt. Contrast NIM (cloud) vs Ollama (local) inference paths.

pages.title: Control Loop & Priority System
pages.purpose: Document the 10 Hz AirSimControlThread._control_loop: the priority ordering (bus mission > spatial guardrail > LLM queue > hover), collision detection and automatic reset flow, telemetry printing cadence, obstacle polling (every 5 ticks), and how the control loop interacts with the MoveQueue. Cover the DroneClient wrapper (move_by_velocity with NED vz negation, move_to_position, get_scene_image, hover) and the constraint that it must only be called from AirSimControlThread.

pages.title: Spatial Guardrail & Coordinate System
pages.purpose: Document the code-enforced safety system and coordinate transformations. Spatial guardrail: max_altitude_hard_m (38 m) triggers 4 m/s descent override, max_patrol_radius_m (400 m) triggers return-to-origin velocity override — both suppress the LLM queue without resetting it. Contrast with the LLM's softer patrol_radius prompt guidance (80% warn threshold). Coordinate system: AirSim NED with Mackenzie Higienópolis origin (lat=-23.5467, lon=-46.6519, alt=780m), ned_to_wgs84 / wgs84_to_ned via pymap3d, vz sign inversion (DroneMove.vz positive=up, AirSim NED z positive=down), and pre-computed bus stop NED targets.

pages.title: Bus Stop Photo Mission
pages.purpose: Document the BusMission state machine (going → arrived → returning → done): how NotifyBusApproach gRPC call enqueues a mission, how the mission saves and restores the LLM move queue around the flight, the arrival radius check, scene image capture via get_scene_image(), BusPhotoPublisher.publish() to Event Hub, TTL expiry, and the bus_queue_max cap. Cover the gRPC server (DroneControllerServicer on port 50052) and the _handle_bus_mission integration in the control loop.

pages.title: gRPC Event Pipeline & Proto Contracts
pages.purpose: Document all event publishers and the inbound gRPC server. Outbound: CrashPublisher (crash events), BusPhotoPublisher (captured scene images), AreaPublisher (Mackenzie area enter/exit with 5 m hysteresis band). Inbound: DroneControllerServicer (receives BusApproachEvent from live-ms-java). Cover the three .proto files (crash_event.proto, bus_event.proto with dual-service pattern, area_event.proto), stub generation via scripts/gen_proto.py, graceful degradation on ImportError, test_mode suppression, and the EventHub endpoint (localhost:50051 outbound, port 50052 inbound).

pages.title: REST API
pages.purpose: Document all FastAPI endpoints in api/rest.py: GET /health, /status, /telemetry, /plan; POST /behavior (immediate replan on behavior override), /start, /stop, /reset, /simulate-bus-approach (testing shortcut). Cover request/response shapes, the AppState attachment pattern via attach_state(), and the DroneBehavior enum values.

pages.title: Configuration & Secrets
pages.purpose: Document the Settings dataclass in config.py: all parameters with defaults and purpose, the Vault KV v2 secret fetch (_fetch_vault_secret for NVIDIA_API_KEY), environment variable fallback pattern, and the DroneBehavior enum. Highlight test_mode (suppresses gRPC), use_ollama toggle (NIM vs local LLM), and key tuning parameters (plan_size, plan_refill_at, guardrail limits, bus TTL). Cover the Windows-specific WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy and the Docker deployment configuration.
